mainEntity.name: What is the average electricity rate in the US?

acceptedAnswer.text: The national average residential electricity rate fluctuates, but recent data shows it hovering around 16 cents per kWh, depending heavily on regional energy sources and grid infrastructure.

mainEntity.name: Which state has the most expensive electricity?

acceptedAnswer.text: Historically, Hawaii has the highest electricity rates due to its reliance on imported petroleum for power generation. Other high-cost states include California and Northeastern states.

mainEntity.name: Why are electricity rates going up?

acceptedAnswer.text: Electricity rates increase due to a combination of factors including the rising cost of natural gas, infrastructure upgrades, extreme weather events stressing the grid, and transition costs to renewable energy.

mainEntity.name: How can I lower my electricity bill?

acceptedAnswer.text: To lower your bill, consider improving home insulation, upgrading to energy-efficient appliances, using a smart thermostat, and shifting heavy energy use to off-peak hours if your utility offers time-of-use rates.

mainEntity.name: Does solar power reduce electricity costs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, installing residential solar panels can significantly reduce monthly electricity bills. The exact savings depend on your local electricity rates, sun exposure, and available incentives or net metering policies.
